# BlazorDX

> A secure-by-default, headless, AOT-safe Blazor component system for .NET 10. MIT-licensed,
> with zero runtime reflection (C# source generators), a Rust/WebAssembly compute tier, and a
> thin TypeScript DOM bridge. ~95 components built on one headless engine. Currently a beta
> preview, built with substantial AI assistance — not intended for production use.

## Full AI context guide
- [blazordx-llms.md](https://blazordx.com/blazordx-llms.md): single-file guide for assistants —
  the analyzer-enforced rules, the source-generator patterns ([GridRow], [DxFormModel]), component
  usage with code snippets, the full catalog, and a minimal end-to-end example. Start here to
  generate correct BlazorDX code.

## Docs
- [Documentation](https://blazordx.com/docs): component catalog with API reference + live demos.
- [FAQ](https://blazordx.com/faq): what it is, license, AOT/trimming, accessibility, install, comparisons.
- [DataGrid](https://blazordx.com/grid): flagship grid — 100k rows, Rust-backed sort/filter/aggregate.
- [Forms + AI](https://blazordx.com/forms): one model renders a form and a Model Context Protocol tool.
- [TicketDesk app](https://blazordx.com/app): a full ITIL service desk built on BlazorDX (real LOB example).

## Project
- [Support](https://blazordx.com/support): community help and commercial support tiers.
- [Privacy](https://blazordx.com/privacy): what the site collects (essentially nothing) and your rights.
- [Source on GitHub](https://github.com/logixrcorp/BlazorDX): MIT-licensed source, issues, discussions, releases.

## Notes
- License: MIT (owned by Logixr Corp).
- Status: beta / AI-generated / not for production use.
- Requires .NET 10.
